items.title: Single perceptron computation graph
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  x1Node["x1"] --> sumNode["z = wTx + b"]
  x2Node["x2"] --> sumNode
  wNode["weights w"] --> sumNode
  bNode["bias b"] --> sumNode
  sumNode --> stepNode["step: 1 if z >= 0 else 0"]
  stepNode --> outNode["binary output y_hat"]
items.caption: One perceptron: weighted sum of inputs plus bias, then threshold (step) activation producing a binary label.

items.title: Linearly separable vs XOR
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  sepNode["linearly separable classes"]
  lineNode["one hyperplane wTx + b = 0 separates them"]
  okNode["perceptron can converge"]
  xorNode["XOR labels in 2D"]
  noLineNode["no single line separates both classes"]
  failNode["single perceptron cannot represent XOR"]
  sepNode --> lineNode --> okNode
  xorNode --> noLineNode --> failNode
items.caption: Separable data admits a hyperplane; XOR proves the limit of a single linear threshold unit.

items.title: Perceptron learning on error
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  predNode["compute y_hat from z = wTx + b"]
  checkNode["y_hat == y?"]
  skipNode["no weight update"]
  updateNode["w <- w + eta*(y - y_hat)*x, b <- b + eta*(y - y_hat)"]
  predNode --> checkNode
  checkNode -->|"yes"| skipNode
  checkNode -->|"no"| updateNode
items.caption: Training only moves the boundary when a labeled point is misclassified; separable data eventually reaches zero training error.
